title: satyrium anthropophorum
intro: Satyrium anthropophorum, also known as the "witch's coker" or "witches' coker", is a species of orchid native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Satyrium anthropophorum include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves or roots in water or using them to make a decoction, which can be consumed as a tea or applied topically.
